incident_summary: API gateway experienced severe latency degradation (p99 latency increased from 0.15s to 18s) due to database connection pool exhaustion caused by a configuration change that tripled HTTP client timeout, lasting approximately 2 hours and 25 minutes at SEV-2 severity

root_cause.summary: HTTP client timeout configuration change from 10s to 30s caused database connection pool exhaustion by holding connections 3x longer, preventing connection recycling
root_cause.trigger: Deployment at 2025-01-15T14:15:00Z increasing HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s
root_cause.mechanism: Tripled timeout duration prevented connection pool from recycling connections fast enough; when pool reached maximum capacity (50/50), new requests queued for up to 30s waiting for available connections, causing cascading latency and timeout errors

customer_impact.description: Customers experienced severe API performance degradation with response times increasing from normal sub-second levels to 15-18 seconds, along with intermittent timeout errors affecting API requests

resolution.action_taken: Emergency rollback of configuration change, reverting HTTP client timeout from 30s back to 10s
resolution.confirmed_by: Metrics showed connection pool utilization dropped from 100% to 60% baseline, p99 latency recovered to 0.18s by 15:00 UTC, 500 errors ceased, CloudWatch logs showed 'Database connection pool recovered' at 15:05 UTC

root_cause: Slack thread engineer confirmation at 2:37 PM and 2:40 PM identifying PR #12345 as cause
GitHub deployment showing timeout change from 10s to 30s at 14:15 UTC
Rollback deployment at 15:45 UTC with description 'Emergency rollback of PR #12345 due to database connection pool exhaustion'
timeline: Prometheus metrics showing p99 latency anomaly start at 14:20 UTC
CloudWatch log recovery message at 15:05 UTC
customer_impact: Prometheus metrics showing p99 latency peak of 18s (120x baseline)
CloudWatch logs showing connection timeout errors
Median latency also degraded to 1.5s (30x baseline)

data_gaps: Scope limited to api-gateway service only - no data on whether other services or regions were affected
No data on percentage of requests affected or total customer count impacted
Timeline discrepancy: metrics show recovery at 15:00 UTC but rollback deployed at 15:45 UTC - unclear why recovery preceded rollback deployment
No data on what specific customer-facing products depend on api-gateway
Connection pool utilization metric shows stable 0.61 baseline in summary but raw values show spike to 1.0 (100%) - inconsistency in metric reporting
